key.name: command
key.description: The application launched as the dropdown — any app, not only foot. Parsed like a shell command line. The window is found via the "app-id" key below, so make sure the command sets that class (e.g. foot --app-id=quake-style-app, or another terminal's --class flag).

key.name: app-id
key.description: The Wayland app-id (or WM_CLASS) of the launched window, used to find and toggle it. Must match what the command above actually sets.

key.name: height-fraction
key.description: Window height as a fraction of the monitor height.

key.name: width-fraction
key.description: Window width as a fraction of the monitor width (centered horizontally).

key.name: position
key.description: Whether the window drops from the top or rises from the bottom.

key.name: bar-position
key.description: Whether the tab bar is above or below the terminal.

key.name: bar-height
key.description: Height of the native tab bar, in logical pixels.

key.name: bar-color
key.description: Background of the tab bar (also the active tab's text color).

key.name: active-color
key.description: Background color of the active tab.

key.name: text-color
key.description: Text color of inactive tabs.

key.name: use-tmux
key.description: When on, the app is launched under tmux so it has tabs (tmux windows). The NewTab/CloseTab/NextTab/PrevTab D-Bus methods control them — bind hotkeys to them in GNOME Settings.

key.name: tmux-session
key.description: The tmux session created for and controlled by the dropdown.

key.name: on-window-close
key.description: What happens when the dropdown window is closed: "kill" ends the tmux session and its processes; "detach" leaves it running in the background so the next open reattaches (typed text / state preserved).

key.name: fill-background
key.description: Draw a solid background across the whole dropdown area, behind the terminal window, so any gap (e.g. foot's cell-quantization on the right) shows this instead of the desktop.

key.name: fill-color
key.description: Color of the background drawn behind the terminal.

key.name: toggle-hotkey
key.description: Keyboard shortcut that shows/hides the dropdown.

key.name: new-tab-hotkey
key.description: Keyboard shortcut that opens a new tab (tmux window).

key.name: close-tab-hotkey
key.description: Keyboard shortcut that closes the current tab.

key.name: next-tab-hotkey
key.description: Keyboard shortcut that switches to the next tab.

key.name: prev-tab-hotkey
key.description: Keyboard shortcut that switches to the previous tab.

key.name: icon
key.description: Icon name (or absolute path) for the dropdown window in Alt+Tab / the dock. Empty = use the launched application's own icon (e.g. foot's), found via its .desktop file.

key.name: tmux-config
key.description: Extra tmux configuration applied ONLY to the dropdown's private, isolated tmux server. The host ~/.tmux.conf is never loaded. One tmux command per line, e.g. "set -g mouse on".
